Subsection.Text: No person shall develop, produce, retain, stockpile, otherwise acquire or possess, use or transfer

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any microbial or other biological agent, or any toxin, for any purpose other than prophylactic, protective or other peaceful purposes; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any weapon, equipment or means of delivery designed to use such an agent or toxin for hostile purposes or in armed conflict.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, subsection (1) does not prohibit any program or activity carried out or authorized by Canada and specifically designed to protect or defend humans, animals or plants against the use of any microbial or other biological agent or toxin for hostile purposes or in armed conflict, or to detect or assess the impact of such use.

Except as authorized under the regulations or any Act of Parliament, no person shall develop, produce, retain, stockpile, otherwise acquire or possess, use or transfer any microbial or other biological agent or toxin identified in the regulations.

Except as authorized under the Export and Import Permits Act or any other Act of Parliament, no person shall export or import a microbial or other biological agent or toxin identified in the regulations made under this Act.
The Minister may designate any person or class of persons to be the responsible authority for the purposes of this Act.
The Minister may designate persons or classes of persons to act as representatives of the responsible authority.

Section.Text: The Minister may designate persons or classes of persons as inspectors for the purpose of the enforcement of this Act, and set conditions applicable to the person's inspection activities, after consulting any other Minister who has powers in relation to inspections for biological agents or toxins.

Subsection.Text: An inspector or a representative of the responsible authority shall be given a certificate of designation, which must state the privileges and immunities applicable to the person and, in the case of an inspector, any conditions applicable under section 9.

An inspector or a representative of the responsible authority shall, on entering any place under this Act, produce the certificate of designation at the request of any individual in charge of that place.
A certificate of designation is not a statutory instrument for the purposes of the Statutory Instruments Act.

Subject to subsection (5), for the purpose of ensuring compliance with this Act, an inspector may enter and inspect, at any reasonable time, any place in which the inspector believes on reasonable grounds there is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any microbial or other biological agent, or any toxin;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any weapon, equipment or means of delivery designed to use such an agent or toxin; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any information relevant to the administration of this Act.

Subsection.Text: An inspector carrying out an inspection may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: require the attendance of and question any person who the inspector considers will be able to assist in the inspection;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: examine, take samples of, detain or remove any thing referred to in subsection (1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: require any person to produce for inspection, or to copy, any document that the inspector believes contains any information relevant to the administration of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: require that any individual in charge of the place take any measures that the inspector considers appropriate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Operation of computer and copying equipment
Subsection.Text: An inspector carrying out an inspection may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use or cause to be used any computer or data processing system to examine any data contained in or available to the computer or system;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reproduce or cause to be reproduced any record from the data, in the form of a printout or other intelligible output, and remove the printout or other output for examination or copying; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: use or cause to be used any equipment at the place to make copies of any data or any record, book of account or other document.

Subsection.Text: An inspector carrying out an inspection may be accompanied by any other person chosen by the inspector.
An inspector may not enter a dwelling-house except with the consent of the occupant or under the authority of a warrant issued under subsection (6).
If on ex parte application a justice of the peace is satisfied by information on oath that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the conditions for entry described in subsection (1) exist in relation to a dwelling-house,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: entry into the dwelling-house is necessary for any purpose relating to the administration of this Act or the regulations, and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: entry into the dwelling-house has been refused or there are reasonable grounds to believe that entry will be refused,

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: the justice may issue a warrant authorizing the inspector named in the warrant to enter the dwelling-house, subject to any conditions that may be specified in the warrant.

Subsection.Text: The inspector may not use force to execute the warrant unless its use is specifically authorized in the warrant.

An inspector is a public officer for the purposes of the application of section 487 of the Criminal Code in respect of an offence under subsection 6(1) unless otherwise provided by the conditions set under section 9.

An inspector may exercise without a warrant any of the powers conferred by virtue of subsection (1) if the conditions for obtaining a warrant exist but, by reason of exigent circumstances, it would not be practical to obtain a warrant.

An inspector who seizes and detains anything shall, as soon as practicable, advise its owner or the person having the possession, care or control of it at the time of its seizure of the reason for the seizure.

No person shall obstruct or hinder, or knowingly make any false or misleading statement either orally or in writing to, an inspector or a representative of the responsible authority engaged in carrying out duties under this Act.

The owner or person in charge of a place entered under section 11 and every person present in that place shall give an inspector all reasonable assistance to enable the inspector to perform his or her duties, and shall furnish the inspector with any information related to the administration of this Act that the inspector reasonably requests.
Except with the authority of an inspector, no person shall remove, alter or interfere in any way with any thing seized under this Act.

Every person who contravenes section 6 or 7 is guilty of an indictable offence and liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $1,000,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding ten years, or to both.

Every person who contravenes section 13 or 17, subsection 18(2) or section 19 or any provision of the regulations is guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable on conviction to a fine not exceeding $50,000 or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding two years, or to both.

Section.Text: Where an offence under this Act is committed or continued on more than one day, the person who committed the offence is liable to be convicted for a separate offence for each day on which the offence is committed or continued.

Subsection.Text: Where a person is alleged to have committed an offence under this Act, proceedings in respect of that offence may be commenced at the instance of the Government of Canada and conducted by the Attorney General of Canada or counsel acting on his or her behalf in any territorial division in Canada, if the offence is alleged to have occurred outside the province in which the proceedings are commenced, whether or not proceedings have previously been commenced elsewhere in Canada.

An accused may be tried and punished in respect of an offence referred to in subsection (1) in the same manner as if the offence had been committed in the territorial division where the proceeding is conducted.

Section.Text: Every person who develops, produces, retains, stockpiles, otherwise acquires or possesses, uses, transfers, exports or imports any microbial or other biological agent, any toxin or any related equipment identified in the regulations shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide such information, at such times and in such form, as may be specified by the regulations, to the responsible authority or to any other portion of the public service of Canada specified by the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: keep and maintain in Canada the documents specified by the regulations, at the person's place of business or at such other place as may be designated by the Minister, in the manner and for the period that is specified by the regulations and, on request by the Minister or the responsible authority, provide the documents to the responsible authority or to any other portion of the public service of Canada specified by the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The Minister may send a notice to any person who the Minister believes on reasonable grounds has information or documents relevant to the enforcement of this Act, requesting the person to provide the information or documents to the Minister.

A person who receives a notice referred to in subsection (1) shall provide the requested information and documents that are under the person's care or control to the Minister in the form and within the time specified in the notice.

Section.Text: No person who obtains information or documents pursuant to this Act or the Convention from a person who consistently treated them in a confidential manner shall knowingly, without the written consent of that person, communicate them or allow them to be communicated to any person, or allow any person to have access to them, except

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of the enforcement or application of this Act or any other Act of Parliament;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: pursuant to an obligation of the Government of Canada under the Convention; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to the extent that they are required to be disclosed or communicated in the interest of public safety.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, on the recommendation of the Minister and any other Minister who has powers in relation to biological agents or toxins, make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: defining biological agent, microbial agent and toxin for the purposes of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting conditions under which activities referred to in subsection 7(1) may be carried on, providing for the issue, suspension and cancellation of authorizations governing the carrying on of any such activity and prescribing the fees or the manner of calculating the fees to be paid in respect of any such authorizations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: identifying microbial or other biological agents and toxins for the purposes of subsection 7(1) or (2);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the powers, privileges, immunities and obligations of representatives of the responsible authority who are designated under subsection 8(2) and respecting the privileges and immunities of inspectors;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the detention, storage, transfer, restoration, forfeiture and disposal — including destruction — of things removed by inspectors under this Act or seized by inspectors under section 487 of the Criminal Code;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 17, identifying microbial or other biological agents and toxins and related equipment, and specifying anything that is to be specified by the regulations; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: generally for carrying out the purposes and provisions of the Convention.

Section.Text: Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by replacing the reference to “subsections 4.8(1) and 6.5(5)” opposite the reference to the Aeronautics Act with a reference to “subsections 4.79(1) and 6.5(5)”.
The definition offence in section 183 of the Criminal Code is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: offence means an offence contrary to, any conspiracy or attempt to commit or being an accessory after the fact in relation to an offence contrary to, or any counselling in relation to an offence contrary to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of this Act, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 77 (endangering safety of aircraft or airport),

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: section 78.1 (offences against maritime navigation or fixed platforms),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.02 (providing or collecting property for certain activities),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.2)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.03 (providing, making available, etc., property or services for terrorist purposes),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.3)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.04 (using or possessing property for terrorist purposes),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.4)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.18 (participation in activity of terrorist group),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.6)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.2 (commission of offence for terrorist group),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.7)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.21 (instructing to carry out activity for terrorist group),

Subparagraph.Label: (xii.8)
Subparagraph.Text: section 83.22 (instructing to carry out terrorist activity),

Subparagraph.Label: (xiii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 96 (possession of weapon obtained by commission of offence),

Subparagraph.Label: (xv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 100 (possession for purpose of weapons trafficking),

Subparagraph.Label: (xvii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 103 (importing or exporting knowing it is unauthorized),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxxvii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 212(2.1) (aggravated offence in relation to living on the avails of prostitution of a person under the age of eighteen years),

Subparagraph.Label: (xxxviii)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 212(4) (offence — prostitution of person under eighteen),

Subparagraph.Label: (xli)
Subparagraph.Text: section 267 (assault with a weapon or causing bodily harm),

Subparagraph.Label: (xlv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 272 (sexual assault with a weapon, threats to a third party or causing bodily harm),

Subparagraph.Label: (li)
Subparagraph.Text: section 282 (abduction in contravention of custody order),

Subparagraph.Label: (liv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 327 (possession of device to obtain telecommunication facility or service),

Subparagraph.Label: (lvi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 342 (theft, forgery, etc., of credit card),

Subparagraph.Label: (lviii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 342.2 (possession of device to obtain computer service),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxiii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 354 (possession of property obtained by crime),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxx)
Subparagraph.Text: section 382 (fraudulent manipulation of stock exchange transactions),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 423.1 (intimidation of justice system participant or journalist),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 424 (threat to commit offences against internationally protected person),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: section 424.1 (threat against United Nations or associated personnel),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxv)
Subparagraph.Text: section 431 (attack on premises, residence or transport of internationally protected person),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxv.1)
Subparagraph.Text: section 431.1 (attack on premises, accommodation or transport of United Nations or associated personnel),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxxiv)
Subparagraph.Text: subsection 462.33(11) (acting in contravention of restraint order),

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxxvi)
Subparagraph.Text: section 467.12 (commission of offence for criminal organization), or

Subparagraph.Label: (lxxxvii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 467.13 (instructing commission of offence for criminal organization),

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: section 198 (fraudulent bankruptcy) of the Bankruptcy and Insolvency Act,

Paragraph.Label: (b.1)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Biological and Toxin Weapons Convention Implementation Act, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 6 (production, etc., of biological agents and means of delivery), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 7 (unauthorized production, etc., of biological agents),

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Competition Act, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 45 (conspiracy) in relation to any of the matters referred to in paragraphs 45(4)(a) to (d) of that Act,

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Controlled Drugs and Substances Act, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: section 3 (bribing a foreign public official) of the Corruption of Foreign Public Officials Act,

Paragraph.Label: (e.1)
Paragraph.Text: the Crimes Against Humanity and War Crimes Act,

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: either of the following provisions of the Customs Act, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Excise Act, 2001, namely,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: section 214 (unlawful production, sale, etc., of tobacco or alcohol),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: section 218 (unlawful possession, sale, etc., of alcohol),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: section 230 (possession of property obtained by excise offences), or

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Export and Import Permits Act, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: any of the following provisions of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act, namely,

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: any offence under the Security of Information Act,

ContinuedDefinition.Text: and includes any other offence that there are reasonable grounds to believe is a criminal organization offence or any other offence that there are reasonable grounds to believe is an offence described in paragraph (b) or (c) of the definition terrorism offence in section 2;

Section.Text: Subsection 81(2) of An Act to amend the Criminal Code (organized crime and law enforcement) and to make consequential amendments to other Acts is repealed.
Section 245 of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act is repealed.

Heading.TitleText: Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act

Subsection.Text: On the later of the coming into force of subsection 36(2) of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act and subsection 13(3) of this Act, subsections 7(7) and (8) of the Aeronautics Act are replaced by the following:
The member of the Tribunal conducting the review may make the following determination:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the decision of the Minister relates to a person's designation under section 4.84, the member may determine the matter by confirming the Minister's decision or by referring the matter back to the Minister for reconsideration; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the decision of the Minister relates to any other Canadian aviation document, the member may determine the matter by confirming the Minister's decision or by substituting his or her own determination.

Subsection.Text: If a decision of the Minister under subsection (1) is referred back to the Minister for reconsideration under paragraph (7)(a), the decision of the Minister remains in effect until the reconsideration is concluded.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Request for reconsideration of immediate threat
Subsection.Text: If no appeal from a determination under subsection (7) confirming the Minister's decision is taken under section 7.2 within the time limited for doing so under that section or an appeal panel has, on an appeal under that section, confirmed the Minister's decision under this section, or if the Minister, after reconsidering the matter under paragraph (7)(a) or 7.2(3)(b), has confirmed the suspension, the holder of the document or the owner or operator of any aircraft, airport or other facility in respect of which the document was issued may, in writing, request the Minister to reconsider whether the immediate threat to aviation safety or security referred to in subsection (1) that occasioned the suspension continues to exist or is likely to occur as described in that subsection.

On the later of the coming into force of section 38 of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act and subsection 14(1) of this Act, section 7.2 of the Aeronautics Act is replaced by the following:
Within thirty days after the determination,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a person affected by the determination may appeal a determination made under subsection 6.72(4), paragraph 7(7)(a) or subsection 7.1(7) to the Tribunal; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a person affected by the determination or the Minister may appeal a determination made under subsection 6.9(8) or paragraph 7(7)(b) to the Tribunal.

Subsection.Text: A party that does not appear at a review hearing is not entitled to appeal a determination, unless they establish that there was sufficient reason to justify their absence.
The appeal panel of the Tribunal assigned to hear the appeal may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under subsection 6.72(4), paragraph 7(7)(a) or subsection 7.1(7), dismiss the appeal or refer the matter back to the Minister for reconsideration; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a determination made under subsection 6.9(8) or paragraph 7(7)(b), dismiss the appeal, or allow the appeal and substitute its own decision.

Subsection.Text: If a decision to suspend or cancel a Canadian aviation document is referred back to the Minister for reconsideration under paragraph (3)(a), the decision of the Minister remains in effect until the reconsideration is concluded. However, the appeal panel, after considering any representations made by the parties, may grant a stay of the decision made under subsection 7.1(7) until the reconsideration is concluded, if it is satisfied that granting a stay would not constitute a threat to aviation safety or security.

On the later of the coming into force of section 38 of the Transportation Appeal Tribunal of Canada Act and subsection 2(4) of this Act, the portion of subsection 3(3) of the Aeronautics Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
The following documents are deemed not to be a Canadian aviation document for the purposes of sections 6.6 to 7.21:
If section 89 of the Pest Control Products Act (the “other Act”), chapter 28 of the Statutes of Canada, 2002, comes into force before section 99 of this Act, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the coming into force of section 89 of the other Act, section 99 of this Act is repealed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the coming into force of section 66 of this Act, the other Act is amended by adding the following after section 67:

Subsection.Text: The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under this Act if the Minister believes that immediate action is required to deal with a significant risk, direct or indirect, to health, safety or the environment.
An interim order has effect from the time that it is made but ceases to have effect on the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 14 days after it is made, unless it is approved by the Governor in Council,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which it is repealed,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which a regulation made under this Act, that has the same effect as the interim order, comes into force, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one year after the interim order is made or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order.

Subsection.Text: No person shall be convicted of an offence consisting of a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention, the person had been notified of the interim order or reasonable steps had been taken to bring the purport of the interim order to the notice of those persons likely to be affected by it.
An interim order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is exempt from the application of sections 3, 5 and 11 of the Statutory Instruments Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall be published in the Canada Gazette within 23 days after it is made.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of any provision of this Act other than this section, any reference to regulations made under this Act is deemed to include interim orders, and any reference to a regulation made under a specified provision of the Act is deemed to include a reference to the portion of an interim order containing any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under the specified provision.
A copy of each interim order must be tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after it is made.
In order to comply with subsection (6), the interim order may be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.

If section 89 of the other Act comes into force after section 99 of this Act, then, on the coming into force of section 89 of the other Act, the other Act is amended by adding the following after section 67:

The Minister may make an interim order that contains any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under this Act if the Minister believes that immediate action is required to deal with a significant risk, direct or indirect, to health, safety or the environment.
An interim order has effect from the time that it is made but ceases to have effect on the earliest of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 14 days after it is made, unless it is approved by the Governor in Council,

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which it is repealed,

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the day on which a regulation made under this Act, that has the same effect as the interim order, comes into force, and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one year after the interim order is made or any shorter period that may be specified in the interim order.

Subsection.Text: No person shall be convicted of an offence consisting of a contravention of an interim order that, at the time of the alleged contravention, had not been published in the Canada Gazette unless it is proved that, at the time of the alleged contravention, the person had been notified of the interim order or reasonable steps had been taken to bring the purport of the interim order to the notice of those persons likely to be affected by it.
An interim order

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is exempt from the application of sections 3, 5 and 11 of the Statutory Instruments Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: shall be published in the Canada Gazette within 23 days after it is made.

Subsection.Text: For the purpose of any provision of this Act other than this section, any reference to regulations made under this Act is deemed to include interim orders, and any reference to a regulation made under a specified provision of the Act is deemed to include a reference to the portion of an interim order containing any provision that may be contained in a regulation made under the specified provision.
A copy of each interim order must be tabled in each House of Parliament within 15 days after it is made.
In order to comply with subsection (6), the interim order may be sent to the Clerk of the House if the House is not sitting.

If section 89 of the other Act comes into force on the same day as section 99 of this Act, then section 89 of the other Act is deemed to have come into force after section 99 of this Act and subsection (2) applies.

Subject to subsection (2), the provisions of this Act, other than sections 1 and 109 to 111.1, and the provisions of any Act that are enacted by this Act come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

The paragraphs or subparagraphs or any other portion of the definition offence in section 183 of the Criminal Code, as enacted by section 108 of this Act, comes into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.

Provision.Text: The person's surname, first name and initial or initials
The person's date of birth
The person's citizenship or nationality or, if not known, the country that issued the travel documents for the person's flight
The person's gender
The number of the person's passport and, if applicable, the number of the person's visa or residency document
The date on which the person's passenger name record was created
If applicable, a notation that the person arrived at the departure gate with a ticket but without a reservation for the flight
If applicable, the names of the travel agency and travel agent that made the person's travel arrangements
The date on which the ticket for the person's flight was issued
If applicable, a notation that the person exchanged their ticket for the flight

The date, if any, by which the person's ticket for the flight had to be paid for to avoid cancellation of the reservation or the date, if any, on which the request for a reservation was activated by the air carrier or person who operates the aviation reservation system
The number assigned to the person's ticket for the flight
If applicable, a notation that the person's ticket for the flight is a one-way ticket
If applicable, a notation that the person's ticket for the flight is valid for one year and is issued for travel between specified points with no dates or flight numbers assigned
The city or country in which the travel included in the person's passenger name record begins
The itinerary cities, being all points where the person will embark or disembark
The name of the operator of the aircraft on which the person is on board or expected to be on board

The names of the operators of aircraft over whose air routes all other segments of air travel included in the person's passenger name record are undertaken, including, for each segment, the name of any operator of aircraft other than the operator that issued the ticket
The code of the operator of the aircraft and the identification number for the person's flight
The person's destination
The travel date for the person's flight
Any seat assignment on the person's flight that was selected for the person before departure
The number of pieces of baggage checked by the person to be carried in the aircraft's cargo compartment on the flight
The tag numbers for the person's baggage
The class of service in respect of the person's flight
Any stated seat request in respect of the person's flight
The person's passenger name record number
The phone numbers of the person and, if applicable, the phone number of the travel agency that made the person's travel arrangements
The person's address and, if applicable, the address of the travel agency that made the travel arrangements
The manner in which the person's ticket was paid for
If applicable, a notation that the person's ticket was paid for by another person
If applicable, a notation that there are gaps in the itinerary included in the person's passenger name record that necessitate travel by an undetermined method
Routing information in respect of the travel included in the person's passenger name record, being the departure and arrival points, codes of the operators of the aircraft, stops and surface segments
If applicable, a notation that the person's ticket is in electronic form and stored electronically in an aviation reservation system
